task_completion.description: Navigate to the Jenkins admin user configuration page to manage API tokens
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins web interface showing the configuration or security page for the 'admin' user where API tokens can be added.

task_completion.description: Create a new API token named 'automation-token'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins UI displaying a newly generated API token with the specific name 'automation-token'.

task_completion.description: Save the generated API token to /home/ga/api_token.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal or text editor showing the token string being written to or saved in the file '/home/ga/api_token.txt'.

task_completion.description: Retrieve the list of Jenkins jobs via the API using the token
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command (e.g., curl, wget, or a script) making an HTTP request to the Jenkins API, using the 'admin' user and the newly created token for authentication.

task_completion.description: Save the API JSON response to /home/ga/jenkins_jobs.json
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command redirecting the API output to '/home/ga/jenkins_jobs.json', or a text editor saving the API response into this file.

task_completion.description: Verify the saved JSON file contains the expected Jenkins jobs
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal (e.g., using cat) or text editor showing the contents of '/home/ga/jenkins_jobs.json' which includes the jobs: 'backend-api-tests', 'frontend-deploy', and 'database-migrations'.

integrity.description: Agent actually generated the token via the Jenkins UI
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the agent interacting with the Jenkins web interface to generate the token, rather than just making up a random string and saving it.

integrity.description: Agent retrieved the jobs via an actual API request
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show an HTTP client (like curl) or script being executed to fetch the data from the Jenkins API, rather than the agent manually typing out a JSON file.

integrity.description: The saved JSON file is a genuine API response
integrity.visual_evidence: The contents of jenkins_jobs.json should resemble a raw Jenkins API response (containing typical Jenkins metadata fields like '_class', 'url', 'color'), not just a manually typed list of the three job names.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected API token name to be created in Jenkins is 'automation-token'. The token value must be saved to '/home/ga/api_token.txt'. The Jenkins jobs list must be retrieved via API and saved to '/home/ga/jenkins_jobs.json'. The expected jobs in the JSON response are: 'backend-api-tests', 'frontend-deploy', and 'database-migrations'.
